paper.title: Empowering Clinical Trial Design through AI: A Randomized Evaluation of PowerGPT
paper.authors: Yiwen Lu, Lu Li, Dazheng Zhang, Xinyao Jian, Tingyin Wang, Siqi Chen, Yuqing Lei, Jiayi Tong, Zhaohan Xi, Haitao Chu, Chongliang Luo, Alexis Ogdie, Brian Athey, Alparslan Turan, Michael Abramoff, Joseph C Cappelleri, Hua Xu, Yun Lu, Jesse Berlin, Daniel I. Sessler, David A. Asch, Xiaoqian Jiang, Yong Chen
paper.note: Supplementary Materials A (containing exact vignette texts) is not included in the arXiv PDF. Tasks 2-8 are reconstructed from the paper's described test types and typical clinical parameters. Task 1 (hip surgery) uses the verbatim vignette from Figure 5.

tasks.title: Hip surgery recovery time
tasks.question: In a large urban hospital, administrators are concerned about patient recovery times after hip surgery due to economic considerations. The hospital head believes the recovery time is 5 days. They plan to conduct a pilot study to determine whether the average recovery time in their hospital differs significantly from the hypothesized value of 5 days. The standard deviation of recovery time is estimated at 2 days. How many patients would need to be included in this study to detect a significant 1-day difference from the hypothesized mean of 5 days, with 80% power and a significance level of 0.05?
tasks.reference_code:
# Cohen's d = delta/sd = 1/2 = 0.5
library(pwr)
ceiling(pwr.t.test(d = 0.5, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80, type = 'one.sample', alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
# 34

tasks.title: Comparing blood pressure between treatments
tasks.question: A cardiologist wants to compare the mean systolic blood pressure reduction between two antihypertensive medications in a randomized controlled trial. Based on previous studies, the expected mean reduction is 8 mmHg with drug A and 5 mmHg with drug B. The common standard deviation is estimated to be 6 mmHg. Equal numbers of patients will be randomized to each group. How many patients are needed per group to detect this 3 mmHg difference with 80% power and a significance level of 0.05 using a two-sided two-sample t-test?
tasks.reference_code:
# Cohen's d = 3/6 = 0.5
library(pwr)
ceiling(pwr.t.test(d = 0.5, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80, type = 'two.sample', alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
# 64
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters

tasks.title: Pre-post cognitive assessment
tasks.question: A neuropsychologist wants to evaluate whether a cognitive training program improves memory scores. Each participant will be tested before and after the 8-week program. Based on pilot data, the expected mean improvement is 4 points with a standard deviation of paired differences of 10 points. How many participants are needed to detect this improvement with 80% power and a significance level of 0.05 using a two-sided paired t-test?
tasks.reference_code:
# Cohen's d = 4/10 = 0.4
library(pwr)
ceiling(pwr.t.test(d = 0.4, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80, type = 'paired', alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
# 52
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters

tasks.title: Comparing three pain management protocols
tasks.question: An anesthesiologist wants to compare post-operative pain scores (0-10 VAS scale) across three different pain management protocols. Based on clinical experience, the expected mean pain scores for the three protocols are 4.0, 3.5, and 3.0, with a common standard deviation of 1.5. How many patients are needed per group to detect differences among the three groups with 80% power at a significance level of 0.05 using a one-way ANOVA?
tasks.reference_code:
# f = sqrt(var(means)/sd^2) = sqrt(var(c(4,3.5,3))/1.5^2)
# var(c(4,3.5,3)) = 0.25, f = sqrt(0.25/2.25) = 0.3333
library(pwr)
ceiling(pwr.anova.test(k = 3, f = sqrt(var(c(4,3.5,3))/1.5^2), sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80)$n)
# 30
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters

tasks.title: Testing new surgical complication rate
tasks.question: A surgeon wants to test whether a new minimally invasive technique has a different complication rate from the known rate of 15% for the standard procedure. The surgeon expects the new technique to have a complication rate of 8%. How many patients are needed to detect this difference with 80% power at a significance level of 0.05 using a two-sided one-proportion z-test?
tasks.reference_code:
# h = 2*asin(sqrt(p1)) - 2*asin(sqrt(p0))
library(pwr)
h <- ES.h(0.08, 0.15)
ceiling(pwr.p.test(h = h, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80, alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
# 160
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters

tasks.title: Comparing treatment response rates
tasks.question: An oncologist plans a randomized trial to compare the response rates of two chemotherapy regimens. The expected response rate for the standard regimen is 30%, and for the experimental regimen is 45%. Equal numbers of patients will be assigned to each group. How many patients are needed per group to detect this difference with 80% power at a significance level of 0.05 using a two-sided two-proportions z-test?
tasks.reference_code:
library(pwr)
h <- ES.h(0.45, 0.30)
ceiling(pwr.2p.test(h = h, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.80, alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
# 162
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters

tasks.title: Comparing survival with new cancer therapy
tasks.question: A clinical researcher is designing a randomized trial to compare overall survival between a new targeted therapy and standard chemotherapy in advanced lung cancer patients. Patients will be randomized 1:1. The expected hazard ratio is 0.75 (new therapy vs. standard). The median survival on standard therapy is 12 months. Patients will be recruited over 24 months and followed for an additional 12 months. Assuming exponential survival, the probability of an event (death) during the study period on the control arm is approximately 0.78, and on the treatment arm is approximately 0.71. How many patients are needed per group to achieve 80% power for a two-sided test at significance level 0.05 using a Cox proportional hazards model?
tasks.reference_code:
library(powerSurvEpi)
ssizeCT.default(power = 0.80, k = 1, pE = 0.71, pC = 0.78, RR = 0.75, alpha = 0.05)
# 259 per group (Freedman's method)
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters using powerSurvEpi

tasks.title: Log-rank test for progression-free survival
tasks.question: A clinical researcher is designing a randomized trial comparing progression-free survival between an immunotherapy combination and standard treatment in metastatic melanoma. Patients will be randomized 1:1. The expected hazard ratio is 0.65 (immunotherapy vs. standard). The probability of an event (progression or death) on the control arm during the study is approximately 0.85, and on the treatment arm is approximately 0.73. How many patients are needed per group to achieve 80% power for a two-sided log-rank test at significance level 0.05?
tasks.reference_code:
library(powerSurvEpi)
ssizeCT.default(power = 0.80, k = 1, pE = 0.73, pC = 0.85, RR = 0.65, alpha = 0.05)
# 111 per group (Freedman's method)
tasks.source_note: Reconstructed from PowerGPT test type parameters using powerSurvEpi
